content:
Press enter or click to view image in full size 6 min read Just now -- Whether you’re preparing for interviews or simply want to refresh your React knowledge, this guide explains the most important React, JavaScript, and AI concepts in the simplest possible way. ✅ Beginner-friendly explanations ✅ Practical interview-focused concepts ✅ Real-world examples ✅ Simple diagrams for quick learning ✅ Easy to revise before interviews 📚 What You’ll Learn 1️⃣ What is React.js? 2️⃣ What is JSX? 3️⃣ What is useState()? 4️⃣ What is useEffect()? 5️⃣ Difference Between var, let and const 6️⃣ What is a Closure? 7️⃣ What is Virtual DOM? 🤖 AI Concepts Every React Developer Should Know 8️⃣ What is an LLM? 9️⃣ What is Prompt Engineering? 🔟 What is RAG? 1️⃣1️⃣ What is MCP? 1️⃣2️⃣ What is Amazon Bedrock? 💡 Before We Begin If you’re new to React, don’t worry. We’ll start with the basics and gradually move toward modern AI concepts used alongside React applications. By the end of this guide, you’ll understand the key ideas needed for interviews and real-world projects. 🏗 React Architecture User │ ▼ React Application │ ┌────────┴────────┐ │ │ ▼ ▼ Components State │ │ └────────┬────────┘ ▼ Virtual DOM ▼ Compare Changes ▼ Real DOM ▼ Updated UI💡 Remember: React updates only what has changed instead of refreshing the entire page.This is one of the main reasons React applications are fast and efficient. 1️⃣ What is React.js? 📌 Easy Definition React is a JavaScript library for building fast, interactive, and reusable user interfaces. Think of React like LEGO blocks. Instead of building the whole website every time, you create small reusable pieces called Components. 🎯 Interview Answer React is an open-source JavaScript library developed by Meta for building reusable, component-based user interfaces using a Virtual DOM for better performance. 💡 Real-life Example Imagine an Amazon product page. Every product card is a React Component. Instead of writing the same HTML 100 times, React reuses one component. 🚀 Remember React = Reusable UI Components 2️⃣ What is JSX? 📌 Easy Definition JSX lets you write HTML-like syntax inside JavaScript, making your components easier to read and maintain function App(){ return

Hello React

; }🚀 Remember JSX = HTML + JavaScript 3️⃣ What is useState()? 📌 Easy Definition useState lets React remember values that can change. Think of it like a notebook. Whenever something changes, React writes the new value and updates the screen automatically. const [count, setCount] = useState(0);🎯 Interview Answer useState is a React Hook used to manage state inside functional components. 🚀 Remember useState = Store changing data 4️⃣ What is useEffect()? useEffect(() => { console.log("Page Loaded"); }, []); 📌 Easy Definition useEffect lets React run code after the component renders. 💡 Examples• Fetch API • Call Backend • Update Page Title 🚀 Remember useEffect = Run side effects 5️⃣ Difference Between var, let and const Press enter or click to view image in full size Example let name = "John"; name = "David"; console.log(name); Output David🚀 Remember Use const by default. Use let when value changes. Avoid var in modern JavaScript. 6️⃣ What is a Closure? 📌 Easy Definition A closure means a function remembers variables from its outer function even after the outer function has finished. function outer(){ let count = 0; return function(){ count++; return count; } } const counter = outer(); console.log(counter()); console.log(counter()); Output 12 🎯 Interview Answer A closure allows an inner function to access variables from its outer function even after the outer function has completed execution. 💡 Real-life Example Imagine locking important documents inside a locker. Only the owner has access. Closures work similarly. 7️⃣ What is Virtual DOM? User Click │ ▼ React updates Virtual DOM │ Compares Old vs New │ Updates Only Changed Part ▼ Browser UI 📌 Easy Definition Virtual DOM is a lightweight copy of the real webpage. React compares changes first before updating the actual screen. This makes React much faster. 🚀 Remember Virtual DOM = Faster UI Updates 🤖 AI Concepts Every React Developer Should Know 8️⃣ What is an LLM? 📌 Easy Definition LLM stands for Large Language Model. It is an AI model trained on a huge amount of text so it can understand and generate human language. Examples • ChatGPT • Claude • Llama • Gemini 🎯 Interview Answer An LLM is an AI model trained on massive datasets to understand and generate human-like text. 💡 Real-life Example Imagine someone who has read millions of books, websites, and documents. An LLM works similarly. It learns patterns in language so it can answer questions, write code, summarize text, and hold conversations. 🚀 Remember LLM = AI Brain 9️⃣ What is Prompt Engineering? 📌 Easy Definition Prompt Engineering means writing clear instructions so AI produces better answers. ❌ Bad Prompt Write React code.✅ Good Prompt Create a React Login Page using Hooks, TypeScript and Material UI.🎯 Better Prompt = Better Result 💡 Think of AI like a new employee. The clearer your instructions, the better the outcome. 🔟 What is RAG? User Question │ ▼Search Company Documents │ ▼Relevant Data Found │ ▼LLM │ ▼Accurate Answer📌 Easy Definition RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) allows an AI model to search external documents before answering. Without RAG The AI answers only from what it has already learned, which may not include the latest or company-specific information. With RAG AI searches first, then answers. 🚀 Remember RAG = Search + AI 💡 Real-life Example Imagine you’re taking an open-book exam. Instead of answering from memory, you first open the textbook, find the correct page, and then answer. That’s exactly how RAG works. 1️⃣1️⃣ What is MCP? AI │ ▼MCP │ ├── GitHub ├── Database ├── Files ├── Slack └── APIs │ ▼Answer📌 Easy Definition Imagine AI as a new employee. The employee knows how to think but cannot open your files, search GitHub, or read your database. MCP acts like a secure bridge that allows AI to use those tools safely. Without MCP AI❌ Cannot access GitHub ❌ Cannot read Database ❌ Cannot open Files With MCP AI↓ MCP ↓ GitHub ↓ Database ↓ Files ↓ Slack ↓ Answer 🎯 Interview Answer MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard that allows AI models to securely communicate with external tools, APIs, databases, and applications using a consistent interface. 🚀 Remember MCP = USB Cable for AI 1️⃣2️⃣ What is Amazon Bedrock? React App |API │AWS Lambda │Amazon Bedrock │Claude / Llama / Nova │AI Response📌 Easy Definition Amazon Bedrock is an AWS service that lets developers use powerful AI models like Claude, Llama, and Amazon Nova without training or managing the models themselves. It also lets you switch between different AI models using a single AWS service, making it easier to build and scale AI applications. 🎯 Interview Answer Amazon Bedrock provides secure access to multiple foundation models through a managed AWS service. 🚀 Remember Bedrock = AWS Platform for Generative AI ⭐ Quick Interview Challenge Q: Why is React faster than traditional DOM manipulation? Answer Because React updates only the changed parts of the page using the Virtual DOM instead of reloading the entire UI. 📌 Key Takeaways ✅ React builds reusable UI components. ✅ JSX lets you write HTML inside JavaScript. ✅ useState manages changing data. ✅ useEffect runs side effects after rendering. ✅ Virtual DOM improves performance. ✅ LLMs generate human-like text. ✅ RAG searches before answering. ✅ MCP connects AI with external tools. ✅ Amazon Bedrock lets you build AI apps without managing models. 🎉 Final Thoughts React is still one of the most in-demand frontend technologies, but modern developers are also expected to understand how AI integrates with web applications. By learning React together with JavaScript fundamentals, LLMs, RAG, Prompt Engineering, MCP, and Amazon Bedrock, you’ll be better prepared for today’s interviews and tomorrow’s real-world projects. ⭐ If this guide helped you, consider bookmarking it and sharing it with another developer who is learning React.
